course_description:
This course gives students an overview of some prevalent developmental disorders including autism, ADHD, sensory disorders, anxiety and mood disorders, and various behaviour disorders. The course covers issues related to diagnosis and assessment and treatment of specific disorders, as well as more general theoretical issues related to causes of disorders in development and the implications of various disorders for the understanding of normal development.

The purpose of this subject is to provide students with an overview of some prevalent childhood developmental disorders.ﾠThe subject will cover issues related to diagnosis, assessment and treatment of specific disorders, as well as more general theoretical issues related to these disorders.

prerequisites: We recommend completing the following courses before enrolling in this one:

course_aims: The course aims to introduce students to the field of developmental disorders of childhood. This will include aetiology, assessment and treatment issues. It is expected that students will become familiar with the features of a wide range of developmental disorders and the cognitive, behavioural, psychological and physical sequelae of these disorders. Students will be introduced to some of the assessment and diagnostic issues surrounding developmental disorders and some basic principles of treatment for selected disorders.

learning_outcomes.description: Define the key features of the developmental disorders that are covered;
Discuss the psychological sequelae for the disorders covered;
Discuss and critically analyze different etiological theories of the disorders covered;
Have some familiarity with classification, assessment and treatment of developmental disorders.
Discuss and critically analyze strengths and weaknesses of diagnostic systems.

assessment_details.title: Tutorial Preparation
assessment_details.task_description: The tutorial program utilises student worksheets with weekly activities and discussion questions. Before each tutorial, students are required to work through activities (e.g., read case studies, watch videos and/or explore relevant websites) and prepare answers for the discussion questions. There is group discussion in tutorials based on the tutorial material released each week. Marks are awarded for attempting the worksheets and submitting them to your tutor prior to or near the start of class. There will be 1 mark for each completed worksheet associated with each of the 9 tutorials and they are not graded. The top 8 completed worksheets will make up the mark, such that students who do not submit 1 worksheet can still receive full marks. The mark out of 8 will be converted to a final mark out of 10. This process builds the necessary skills to complete the Case Study assignment. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
If you miss participation or worksheets within a tutorial you should contact your tutor and/or course coordinator.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Tutorial Quizzes
assessment_details.task_description: Please bring a laptop or phone to complete the quizzes in tutorials. The objective of this piece of assessment is to assess your knowledge and understanding of the lecture content, tutorial and text readings relating to developmental disorders of childhood. You will be given a 12-item multiple choice quiz which will assess your knowledge for the preceding classes. You will have 20 minutes to complete each quiz and you will only be able to take each quiz once. You will complete the quiz in your tutorial. There are no extensions. Each multiple choice item will have four options, with only one correct option. You will receive a mark out of 12 for each of the 4 quizzes, for a total of 48 marks. Your mark will then be based on your best 3 quizzes, so you can miss 1 quiz with no penalty. This score will then be converted to a final mark out of 40 for this component. The four quizzes will be conducted on: Quiz 1 (corresponding to lecture Topic 1 and 2): Week 4 Tutorial Quiz 2 (corresponding lecture Topic 3, 4 and 5): Week 7 Tutorial Quiz 3 (corresponding lecture Topic 6 and 7): Week 9 Tutorial Quiz 4 (corresponding lecture Topic 8, 9 and 10): Week 12 Tutorial Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Only 3 out of the 4 quizzes count towards your final mark. You can miss 1 quizz and still receive full marks for this piece of assessment.
We strongly encourage students experiencing significant interruptions or chronic illnesses of greater than two weeks to contact UQ Student Services to seek information and/or assistance: http://www.uq.edu.au/student-services/
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Individual Case Report
assessment_details.task_description: This 2000 word report is based on a case study and requires students to identify a disorder or disorders and justify the diagnosis on the basis of knowledge of symptomatology. Students need to be able to describe further assessment information that they would require in the case and also need to be able to explain how this disorder may have developed and how it is currently being maintained. Finally, students will be asked to outline a treatment plan for the case in question. Students will have many opportunities within tutorials to develop the skills and knowledge necessary to complete this assignment. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
submit via Turnitin NOTE: You will no longer receive an automatic submission receipt from Turnitin confirming your assignment has been received. You should download a copy of the digital receipt from the Assignment Dashboard and keep this as a record.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
For full details please review the School of Psychology Student Guidelines - Extensions and Deferred quiz/exam (the web link can be found on the Policies and Procedures tab).
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
